UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the TaskMind application, primarily for associating tasks. Authentication details are managed externally via Google Sign-In.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID typically maps to an external authentication system's user ID.

email.description: The user's email address, often retrieved from the authentication provider, used for display purposes.

displayName.description: The user's preferred display name or full name, typically provided by the authentication system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile record was last updated.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a single to-do item for a user in the TaskMind application, including its status, details, and nested subtasks.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who owns this task.

description.description: The detailed description or title of the task.

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the task has been marked as completed.

subtasks.description: A list of smaller, actionable steps derived from the main task.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task record was last updated.

dueDate.description: An optional due date for when the task should be completed.

priority.description: The priority level of the task (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores individual tasks associated with a specific user, including nested subtasks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this task.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific task.

firestore.reasoning: Tasks are stored as subcollections of users to ensure path-based ownership and isolation. Subtasks are stored as an array within the Task document for atomic updates and simple management of task-specific hierarchies.
